activity.title: Initial Finishing Challenge
activity.setup: Set up a small-sided game in the final third with goals and live defenders. Use cones to mark wide zones. Restarts occur from the goalkeeper.
activity.teams: Two teams compete in final third attacking and defending; the attacking team creates and converts scoring chances under live defensive pressure while the defending team contests finishing opportunities and counter-attacks immediately from clearances. Player structure logic: Even or attacking overload.
activity.objective: Players choose when to exploit wide zones and decide the best moment to shoot or pass under pressure from defenders. Session focus: Create and convert scoring chances.
rules: If a team recognizes the live opportunity created by Wide Zone Advantage, then they exploit it under pressure, keep the next action live, and gain the selected advantage; but if they force the action into pressure or lose the ball while the picture is closed, then the opponent gains the connected restart, regain, or counter-attacking advantage, and play continues live inside the Small Area Condition.
Final third context — all game actions take place in or around the scoring area under live defensive pressure.
Live defenders contest every finishing attempt; clearances and saves create immediate counter-attack opportunities.
activity.scoring: A goal or live advantage counts only when the team creates a genuine scoring chance under live defensive pressure and converts it. Scoring must be tied to genuine finishing chances created and converted under live defensive pressure. Players face a decision — shoot, cut inside, or hold for a better angle based on goalkeeper position and defensive cover. Rules and scoring must create live finishing situations under defensive contest; success must reward genuine chance creation and conversion. If the live opportunity is forced after it closes, the opponent inherits the connected advantage immediately.
constraints: Small Area Condition: Compact pitch proportions place teams in tight space with reduced time.
Wide Zone Advantage: Provide scoring or positional advantage in wide areas.
Progression Bonus: Reward advancement into attacking areas through successful progression.
coachingFocus: Watch how players read goalkeeper position and defensive cover before deciding.
Focus on the live decisions — how players read pressure, space, and support, and how they adapt when the picture changes.
Players read goalkeeper position, defensive cover, and angle of approach before deciding to shoot, cut inside, or hold for a better chance.
